danger_signals.visual_form: Harry and Marv character sprites in side-scrolling sections
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat engagement; triggers pain meter challenge

danger_signals.visual_form: Undistinguished van visible in top-down neighborhood view
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent house robbery; player must travel to intercept before bandits enter

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual indication of house becoming 'flooded' after successful bandit robbery
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent loss of that house; failure state for that location

danger_signals.visual_form: Unfilled meter during platformer combat sections
danger_signals.meaning: Kevin has not yet dealt sufficient damage; bandits will complete robbery

safety_signals.visual_form: Houses in neighborhood view that remain dry/unflooded
safety_signals.meaning: Successfully defended location; bandits have retreated
Sufficient damage dealt; bandits forced to retreat from that house

safety_signals.visual_form: Items scattered in environment that combine into crafted weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for defense; increased combat capability
Police arrival imminent; endurance near completion; safety approaching

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: BB gun starting weapon; makeshift crafted weapons
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kevin vs. Harry and Marv in side-scrolling combat sections
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traversal between houses via sled in top-down view; presence in houses during defense phases

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Undistinguished van; water left running as calling card (visual flooding effect)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Two distinct bandit sprites in platformer sections; van sprite in top-down neighborhood view
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Flooded houses indicate their successful robberies; presence in houses triggers combat mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Top-down view of neighborhood with multiple houses; van moving through streets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin travels via sled; Wet Bandits drive van and select houses to rob

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift to 2D side-scrolling platformer perspective; Kevin and Bandits visible in house interior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat engagement with pain meter; bandits proceed to rob while Kevin damages them

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: House remains unflooded in neighborhood view; visual distinction from compromised locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bandits retreat; van departs to find new target

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: House shows flooding/water damage effect in neighborhood view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: House becomes inaccessible; cannot be re-entered; counts toward game over condition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer approaching 20 minutes (or 40 on harder difficulty)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased urgency; continued defense attempts until time expires and police arrive

environmental_storytelling.example: Marv leaves water running in robbed houses; visual flooding becomes signature of bandit success and player failure in that location
Various items scattered throughout houses that can be combined into crafted weapons; communicates that household objects are defense tools
Multiple houses in neighborhood view establish stakes—each location is a potential loss; spatial layout communicates scope of protection responsibility
Undistinguished van driving through neighborhood; visual representation of threat movement and unpredictability of bandit target selection

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Van travels through neighborhood; periodically select houses to enter for robbery
Enter side-scrolling house interior; proceed through space while accumulating robbery meter; respond to Kevin's damage with knockback
Retreat from house; van returns to neighborhood patrol
Engages in platformer combat; uses weapons to fill pain meter; must fill it before robbery meter completes
Sled traversal in top-down neighborhood view; responds to van presence by moving toward threatened houses

notes: Home Alone Genesis features a dual-view system communicating world state through perspective shifts: top-down neighborhood patrolling represents macro-level threat distribution, while side-scrolling house interiors represent localized combat intensity. The Pain Meter vs. Robbery Meter dynamic creates visual tension between player success (filling pain meter) and failure condition (robbery meter completion). Flooded houses serve as persistent visual failure markers, escalating pressure as more locations become inaccessible. The timer countdown is the primary meta-signal for approaching safety (police arrival). Weapon crafting from scattered items establishes environmental agency and resource management as core mechanical-narrative elements.
